summary: Good‑quality tape of the July 2 1989 Buffalo show with a rare Playin → Crazy Fingers opener and a memorable Playin reprise, plus strong Eyes of the World and drum work.
review: Both reviewers rave about the unusual set‑opener (Playing in the Band into Crazy Fingers) and the later Playin reprise that emerged from Ship of Fools, calling the performance “great” and noting a Hendrix‑style drum solo on Eyes of the World. No recording flaws are mentioned, suggesting a clear, listenable source even though its exact type isn’t specified.

ai_rating.rationale: Two detailed reviews give high marks (4‑5 stars) and highlight several standout moments, while recording quality is unremarkable but not criticized.

recording_quality.technical_notes: No complaints about sound; reviewers imply the tape faithfully captures the performance.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Unusual but exciting set opener (Playin → Crazy Fingers) followed by a later Playin reprise from Ship of Fools, creating a memorable arc praised by fans.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Mentioned in the title as greeting the crowd (“how you all doing”) and implied to be part of the lively vibe.
band_member_comments.Drums: A Hendrix‑style riff during Eyes of the World is highlighted, indicating standout drum work.
